query: SPC storm reports Iowa tornadoes May 2026 daily reports
answer:
Now I have comprehensive data. Let me compile the final research report.

---

# SPC Storm Reports: Iowa Tornadoes — May 2026 (Daily Reports)

## Summary

Based on data retrieved directly from the NOAA Storm Prediction Center (SPC) filtered and unfiltered tornado CSV reports for May 1–24, 2026, **Iowa recorded tornado reports on 4 distinct days in May 2026**, with a total of approximately **24 filtered tornado report entries** (some representing the same tornado track with multiple reports). The most active day by far was the overnight period of **May 17 (UTC)**, which produced a major tornado outbreak across northwest and north-central Iowa.

---

## Day-by-Day Breakdown of Iowa Tornado Reports

### May 16, 2026 — 1 filtered report (2 unfiltered)
| Time (UTC) | Location | County | Lat | Lon | Comments |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2251 | 1 SW Gravity | Taylor | 40.75 | -94.76 | Via Spotter Network (DMX) |
| 2254* | 1 SE Gravity | Taylor | 40.75 | -94.73 | Unfiltered only (DMX) |

- A tornado was reported near Gravity in Taylor County (southwest Iowa) as part of a broader severe weather event across the central Plains. The unfiltered data shows a second report just 3 minutes later at a nearby location, likely the same tornado continuing east.

### May 17, 2026 — 18 filtered reports (20 unfiltered) **[Major Outbreak]**
This was the most significant tornado day for Iowa in May 2026. Reports span from **0031 UTC to 0327 UTC** (approximately 7:31 PM to 10:27 PM CDT on the evening of May 17), with tornadoes tracking across **13 different Iowa counties** from the far western border to north-central Iowa.

| Time (UTC) | Location | County | Key Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0031 | Salix | Woodbury | Emergency manager report (FSD) |
| 0035 | 3 NE Sergeant Bluff | Woodbury | Brief gustnado, multiple social media videos (FSD) |
| 0057 | 5 NW Kingsley | Plymouth | Kingsley Fire Dept report (FSD) |
| 0112 | 7 S Marcus | Cherokee | Video of brief gustnado (FSD) |
| 0125 | 3 W Cherokee | Cherokee | Brief gustnado, social media videos (FSD) |
| 0129 | 3 NE Meriden | Cherokee | Brief tornado, 2–3 min, confirmed by radar (FSD) |
| 0131 | 5 WSW Royal | Clay | Emergency manager relayed video (FSD) |
| 0201 | Pomeroy | Calhoun | Fire dept report (DMX) |
| 0205 | 2 SSE Havelock | Pocahontas | Between Pocahontas and Havelock (DMX) |
| 0208 | 2 NW Terril | Dickinson | Viewed on social media (FSD) |
| 0214 | Graettinger | Palo Alto | Power pole and tree damage (DMX) |
| 0227 | Moorland | Webster | Trees >8" diameter downed (DMX) |
| 0228 | 3 SSW Armstrong | Emmet | Confirmed via webcam (DMX) |
| 0238 | 2 S Wesley | Kossuth | Confirmed by Emergency Management (DMX) |
| 0259 | 4 N Crystal Lake | Winnebago | Power line sparking/flashing (DMX) |
| 0310 | 1 W Joice | Worth | **Structure damage** (DMX) |
| 0321 | 3 S Mason City | Cerro Gordo | Fire reported tornado on ground (DMX) |
| 0327 | 3 S Mason City | Cerro Gordo | Fire confirmed tornado on ground (DMX) |

**Counties affected (13):** Woodbury, Plymouth, Cherokee, Clay, Calhoun, Pocahontas, Dickinson, Palo Alto, Webster, Emmet, Kossuth, Winnebago, Worth, Cerro Gordo

**Track:** The overall pattern shows tornadoes moving from west-northwest Iowa (Woodbury Co, near Sioux City) eastward and northeastward across northwest and north-central Iowa, reaching Mason City (Cerro Gordo Co) by ~10:27 PM CDT. This is consistent with a QLCS (quasi-linear convective system) or supercell complex tracking across Iowa.

### May 18, 2026 — 4 filtered reports
| Time (UTC) | Location | County | Key Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2330 | 5 WNW Athelstan | Taylor | Trained spotter confirms (DMX) |
| 2340 | 5 WNW Athelstan | Taylor | Trained spotter confirms (DMX) |
| 2356 | Benton | Ringgold | Emergency manager confirms (DMX) |
| 0009 | 5 N Mount Ayr | Ringgold | Multiple EM reports of confirmed tornado (DMX) |

- Activity concentrated in **Taylor and Ringgold counties** (extreme southwest Iowa). The two Athelstan reports likely represent the same tornado observed 10 minutes apart. The tornado(es) tracked from Taylor County eastward into Ringgold County and past Mount Ayr.

### May 22, 2026 — 1 filtered report
| Time (UTC) | Location | County | Key Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2216 | 3 NNE Oto | Woodbury | Very brief touchdown, documented by two independent photos (FSD) |

- A brief tornado near Oto in Woodbury County (western Iowa).

### May 1–15, 19–21, 23–24: No Iowa tornado reports

---

## Key Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total days with Iowa tornado reports (filtered) | 4 |
| Total Iowa filtered tornado report entries | 24 |
| Total Iowa unfiltered tornado report entries | 27 |
| Peak day (by report count) | May 17 (18 filtered reports) |
| Counties with tornado reports | ~15 unique counties |
| NWS offices issuing Iowa reports | DMX (Des Moines), FSD (Sioux Falls) |
| Most active region | NW & N-Central Iowa (May 17 outbreak) |
| Reports with noted damage | Graettinger (power poles/trees), Moorland (large trees), Joice (structures), Mason City (confirmed on ground) |

---

## Notes on Data Quality

- **Filtered vs. Unfiltered:** The SPC "filtered" data removes duplicate reports for the same tornado event. The unfiltered data contains 3 additional reports (1 for May 16, 2 for May 17), likely duplicates.
- **EF-Scale ratings:** All Iowa tornado reports show "UNK" (unknown) for the F_Scale column, indicating that formal NWS damage surveys may still be pending or that the events were too brief for formal classification as of the data retrieval date.
- **Gustnado vs. Tornado:** Several of the May 17 reports (e.g., Sergeant Bluff, Marcus, Cherokee) are described as "gustnadoes" — these are technically not classified as tornadoes by the NWS but appear in the tornado report feed. The filtered count still includes them.
- **UTC timing:** SPC reports use UTC time. The May 17 reports timestamped 0031–0327 UTC correspond to ~7:31–10:27 PM CDT on the evening of May 17.

key_facts: Iowa had tornado reports on 4 days in May 2026 (May 16, 17, 18, and 22) based on SPC filtered storm reports through May 24.

May 17, 2026 was the most active day with 18 filtered tornado report entries across 13+ Iowa counties, representing a significant outbreak from western Iowa (Woodbury/Sioux City area) to north-central Iowa (Mason City/Cerro Gordo County).
The May 17 outbreak tornadoes tracked from ~0031 UTC to 0327 UTC (7:31-10:27 PM CDT), consistent with a QLCS or supercell complex moving west to east across northwest and north-central Iowa.
May 18, 2026 had 4 filtered tornado reports in Taylor and Ringgold counties (extreme SW Iowa), with confirmed tornadoes near Athelstan, Benton, and Mount Ayr.
May 16, 2026 had 1 filtered tornado report near Gravity in Taylor County (SW Iowa).
May 22, 2026 had 1 filtered tornado report of a very brief touchdown near Oto in Woodbury County, documented by two independent photos.
All Iowa tornado reports in May 2026 carry an 'UNK' EF-scale rating, suggesting formal NWS damage surveys may be pending.
Reports were issued by two NWS offices: DMX (Des Moines) and FSD (Sioux Falls, SD).
Several May 17 reports describe 'gustnadoes' rather than true tornadoes, which may be reclassified upon further review.
Damage was noted in several locations including structure damage in Joice (Worth Co.), power pole/tree damage in Graettinger (Palo Alto Co.), and large trees downed in Moorland (Webster Co.).
Total unfiltered Iowa tornado report entries for May 2026: 27; total filtered: 24.
Days with zero Iowa tornado reports: May 1-15, May 19-21, May 23-24.
